user_query:
Hey! Since I’m on the road a lot, I need our tiny demo project to have an offline-friendly CI routine I can run from my laptop. Please do the following in this workspace and produce the artifacts under output/ so I can share them before the next show:

1) Modify the pipeline config at ci/pipeline.json so that:
   - It sets a top-level environment variable APP_ENV to "prod".
   - It keeps the existing test stage as-is.
   - It adds a new build stage that runs after the test stage and packages the app directory into a zip at output/build/package.zip by executing: python tools/package.py --src app --out output/build/package.zip

2) Run the local CI simulation and capture logs:
   - Execute: python simulate_ci.py --pipeline ci/pipeline.json --log output/ci.log
   - The run should complete successfully. On success, you should see all of the following created:
     - output/ci.log (the full pipeline log)
     - output/test_results.json (JSON summary from tools/run_tests.py)
     - output/build/package.zip (zipped app build artifact)

3) Draft a short status email I can paste to our tour manager:
   - Save it to output/rollout_email.txt
   - Include a single-line Subject starting with "Subject: CI Update —".
   - In the body, explicitly mention the env var set as APP_ENV=prod.
   - Include a line that states the pipeline status exactly as: "Pipeline status: SUCCESS".
   - Include a summary line with test counts parsed from output/test_results.json in the format: tests_run: <N>, tests_passed: <N>, tests_failed: <N>.
   - Include the artifact path: output/build/package.zip.
   - Keep it concise and friendly—imagine I’m sending it from the venue.

Do not rely on any network access. Everything should run locally. Deliverables to verify:
- Updated ci/pipeline.json with the env and build stage.
- The three files: output/ci.log, output/test_results.json, output/build/package.zip.
- The drafted message at output/rollout_email.txt that includes the exact phrases and fields listed above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
